steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: Before the blank, “plastic-bag consumption decreased by up to ninety” is an independent clause. After the blank, “taxes are subject to what economists call the 'rebound effect'” is also an independent clause. The word “Such” refers back to “taxes” and begins the second clause.

steps.title: Apply the separation rule
steps.content: Two independent clauses require a period, semicolon, or comma with a coordinating conjunction—option C uses a period correctly, allowing “Such taxes” to begin a new sentence. Options A, B, and D fail by creating a comma splice, using improper “and such” phrasing, and creating a run-on, respectively. That matches choice C — C is correct.
